L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Sept. 2, 1974Oct. 22, 1986“In the case of any plan maintained on , if, not later than 2 years after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the plan administrator petitions the Secretary of Labor, the Secretary of Labor may prescribe an alternate method which shall be treated as satisfying the requirements of subsection (a)(2) of section 411 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954], or of subsection (b)(1) (other than subparagraph (D) thereof) of such section 411, or of both such provisions for a period of not more than 4 years. The Secretary may prescribe such alternate method only when he finds that—January 1, 1974Sept. 2, 1974“(1)the application of such requirements would increase the costs of the plan to such an extent that there would result a substantial risk to the voluntary continuation of the plan or a substantial curtailment of benefit levels or the levels of employees’ compensation,“(2)the application of such requirements or discontinuance of the plan would be adverse to the interests of plan participants in the aggregate, and“(3)a waiver or extension of time granted under [former] section 412(d) or (e) would be inadequate.In the case of any plan with respect to which an alternate method has been prescribed under the preceding provisions of this subsection for a period of not more than 4 years, if, not later than 1 year before the expiration of such period, the plan administrator petitions the Secretary of Labor for an extension of such alternate method, and the Secretary makes the findings required by the preceding sentence, such alternate method may be extended for not more than 3 years.”

children.heading: Employee contributions
children.label: 1 Employee contributions
children.text: A plan satisfies the requirements of this paragraph if an employee’s rights in his accrued benefit derived from his own contributions are nonforfeitable.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of a defined benefit plan, a plan satisfies the requirements of this paragraph if it satisfies the requirements of clause (ii) or (iii).

children.heading: 5-year vesting
children.label: ii 5-year vesting
children.text: A plan satisfies the requirements of this clause if an employee who has completed at least 5 years of service has a nonforfeitable right to 100 percent of the employee’s accrued benefit derived from employer contributions.

children.heading: 3 to 7 year vesting
children.label: iii 3 to 7 year vesting
children.text: A plan satisfies the requirements of this clause if an employee has a nonforfeitable right to a percentage of the employee’s accrued benefit derived from employer contributions determined under the following table:Years of service:The nonforfeitable percentage is:3204405606807 or more100.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of a defined contribution plan, a plan satisfies the requirements of this paragraph if it satisfies the requirements of clause (ii) or (iii).

children.heading: 3-year vesting
children.label: ii 3-year vesting
children.text: A plan satisfies the requirements of this clause if an employee who has completed at least 3 years of service has a nonforfeitable right to 100 percent of the employee’s accrued benefit derived from employer contributions.

children.heading: 2 to 6 year vesting
children.label: iii 2 to 6 year vesting
children.text: A plan satisfies the requirements of this clause if an employee has a nonforfeitable right to a percentage of the employee’s accrued benefit derived from employer contributions determined under the following table:Years of service:The nonforfeitable percentage is:2203404605806 or more100.

children.heading: Forfeiture on account of death
children.label: A Forfeiture on account of death
children.text: A right to an accrued benefit derived from employer contributions shall not be treated as forfeitable solely because the plan provides that it is not payable if the participant dies (except in the case of a survivor annuity which is payable as provided in section 401(a)(11)).

children.heading: Suspension of benefits upon reemployment of retiree
children.label: B Suspension of benefits upon reemployment of retiree
i
children.text: in the case of a plan other than a multi-employer plan, by the employer who maintains the plan under which such benefits were being paid; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a multiemployer plan, in the same industry, the same trade or craft, and the same geographic area covered by the plan as when such benefits commenced.

children.heading: Effect of retroactive plan amendments
children.label: C Effect of retroactive plan amendments
children.text: A right to an accrued benefit derived from employer contributions shall not be treated as forfeitable solely because plan amendments may be given retroactive application as provided in section 412(d)(2).

children.label: i
children.text: A right to an accrued benefit derived from employer contributions shall not be treated as forfeitable solely because the plan provides that, in the case of a participant who does not have a nonforfeitable right to at least 50 percent of his accrued benefit derived from employer contributions, such accrued benefit may be forfeited on account of the withdrawal by the participant of any amount attributable to the benefit derived from mandatory contributions (as defined in subsection (c)(2)(C)) made by such participant.

children.label: ii
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to a plan unless the plan provides that any accrued benefit forfeited under a plan provision described in such clause shall be restored upon repayment by the participant of the full amount of the withdrawal described in such clause plus, in the case of a defined benefit plan, interest. Such interest shall be computed on such amount at the rate determined for purposes of subsection (c)(2)(C) on the date of such repayment (computed annually from the date of such withdrawal). The plan provision required under this clause may provide that such repayment must be made (I) in the case of a withdrawal on account of separation from service, before the earlier of 5 years after the first date on which the participant is subsequently re-employed by the employer, or the close of the first period of 5 consecutive 1-year breaks in service commencing after the withdrawal; or (II) in the case of any other withdrawal, 5 years after the date of the withdrawal.

children.label: iii
children.text: In the case of accrued benefits derived from employer contributions which accrued before , a right to such accrued benefit derived from employer contributions shall not be treated as forfeitable solely because the plan provides that an amount of such accrued benefit may be forfeited on account of the withdrawal by the participant of an amount attributable to the benefit derived from mandatory contributions (as defined in subsection (c)(2)(C)) made by such participant before if such amount forfeited is proportional to such amount withdrawn. This clause shall not apply to any plan to which any mandatory contribution is made after . The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this clause.September 2, 1974September 2, 1974September 2, 1974

children.label: iv
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, in the case of any class-year plan, a withdrawal of employee contributions shall be treated as a withdrawal of such contributions on a plan year by plan year basis in succeeding order of time.

children.label: v
children.text: For nonforfeitability where the employee has a nonforfeitable right to at least 50 percent of his accrued benefit, see section 401(a)(19).

children.heading: Cessation of contributions under a multiemployer plan
children.label: E Cessation of contributions under a multiemployer plan
children.text: A right to an accrued benefit derived from employer contributions under a multiemployer plan shall not be treated as forfeitable solely because the plan provides that benefits accrued as a result of service with the participant’s employer before the employer had an obligation to contribute under the plan may not be payable if the employer ceases contributions to the multi­employer plan.

children.heading: Reduction and suspension of benefits by a multiemployer plan
children.label: F Reduction and suspension of benefits by a multiemployer plan
i
children.text: the plan is amended to reduce benefits under section 4281 of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, or

children.label: ii
children.text: benefit payments under the plan may be suspended under section 418E or under section 4281 of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974.

children.heading: Treatment of matching contributions forfeited by reason of excess deferral or contribution or permissible withdrawal
children.label: G Treatment of matching contributions forfeited by reason of excess deferral or contribution or permissible withdrawal
children.text: A matching contribution (within the meaning of section 401(m)) shall not be treated as forfeitable merely because such contribution is forfeitable if the contribution to which the matching contribution relates is treated as an excess contribution under section 401(k)(8)(B), an excess deferral under section 402(g)(2)(A), a permissible withdrawal under section 414(w), or an excess aggregate contribution under section 401(m)(6)(B).

children.heading: Service included in determination of nonforfeitable percentage
children.label: 4 Service included in determination of nonforfeitable percentage
A
children.text: years of service before age 18;

children.label: B
children.text: years of service during a period for which the employee declined to contribute to a plan requiring employee contributions;

children.label: C
children.text: years of service with an employer during any period for which the employer did not maintain the plan or a predecessor plan (as defined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary);

children.label: D
children.text: service not required to be taken into account under paragraph (6);

children.label: E
children.text: years of service before , unless the employee has had at least 3 years of service after ;January 1, 1971December 31, 1970

children.label: F
children.text: years of service before the first plan year to which this section applies, if such service would have been disregarded under the rules of the plan with regard to breaks in service as in effect on the applicable date; and

children.label: I
children.text: a complete withdrawal of that employer from the plan (within the meaning of section 4203 of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974), or

children.label: II
children.text: to the extent permitted in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, a partial withdrawal described in section 4205(b)(2)(A)(i) of such Act in conjunction with the decertification of the collective bargaining representative, and

children.label: ii
children.text: with any employer under the plan after the termination date of the plan under section 4048 of such Act.

children.heading: General rule
children.label: A General rule
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, except as provided in subparagraph (C), the term “year of service” means a calendar year, plan year, or other 12-consecutive month period designated by the plan (and not prohibited under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Labor) during which the participant has completed 1,000 hours of service.

children.heading: Hours of service
children.label: B Hours of service
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “hours of service” has the meaning provided by section 410(a)(3)(C).

children.heading: Seasonal industries
children.label: C Seasonal industries
children.text: In the case of any seasonal industry where the customary period of employment is less than 1,000 hours during a calendar year, the term “year of service” shall be such period as may be determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Labor.

children.heading: Maritime industries
children.label: D Maritime industries
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, in the case of any maritime industry, 125 days of service shall be treated as 1,000 hours of service. The Secretary of Labor may prescribe regulations to carry out the purposes of this subparagraph.

children.heading: Definition of 1-year break in service
children.label: A Definition of 1-year break in service
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “1-year break in service” means a calendar year, plan year, or other 12-consecutive-month period designated by the plan (and not prohibited under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Labor) during which the participant has not completed more than 500 hours of service.

children.heading: 1 year of service after 1-year break in service
children.label: B 1 year of service after 1-year break in service
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (4), in the case of any employee who has any 1-year break in service, years of service before such break shall not be required to be taken into account until he has completed a year of service after his return.

children.heading: 5 consecutive 1-year breaks in service under defined contribution plan
children.label: C 5 consecutive 1-year breaks in service under defined contribution plan
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (4), in the case of any participant in a defined contribution plan, or an insured defined benefit plan which satisfies the requirements of subsection (b)(1)(F), who has 5 consecutive 1-year breaks in service, years of service after such 5-year period shall not be required to be taken into account for purposes of determining the nonforfeitable percentage of his accrued benefit derived from employer contributions which accrued before such 5-year period.

children.label: I
children.text: 5, or

children.label: II
children.text: the aggregate number of years of service before such period.

children.heading: Years of service not taken into account
children.label: ii Years of service not taken into account
children.text: If any years of service are not required to be taken into account by reason of a period of breaks in service to which clause (i) applies, such years of service shall not be taken into account in applying clause (i) to a subsequent period of breaks in service.

children.heading: Nonvested participant defined
children.label: iii Nonvested participant defined
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the term “nonvested participant” means a participant who does not have any nonforfeitable right under the plan to an accrued benefit derived from employer contributions.

children.heading: Special rule for maternity or paternity absences
children.label: E Special rule for maternity or paternity absences
I
children.text: by reason of the pregnancy of the individual,

children.label: II
children.text: by reason of the birth of a child of the individual,

children.label: III
children.text: by reason of the placement of a child with the individual in connection with the adoption of such child by such individual, or

children.label: IV
children.text: for purposes of caring for such child for a period beginning immediately following such birth or placement,

children.heading: Hours treated as hours of service
children.label: ii Hours treated as hours of service
I
children.text: the hours of service which otherwise would normally have been credited to such individual but for such absence, or

children.label: II
children.text: in any case in which the plan is unable to determine the hours described in subclause (I), 8 hours of service per day of absence,

children.heading: Year to which hours are credited
children.label: iii Year to which hours are credited
I
children.text: only in the year in which the absence from work begins, if a participant would be prevented from incurring a 1-year break in service in such year solely because the period of absence is treated as hours of service as provided in clause (i); or

children.label: II
children.text: in any other case, in the immediately following year.

children.heading: Year defined
children.label: iv Year defined
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “year” means the period used in computations pursuant to paragraph (5).

children.heading: Information required to be filed
children.label: v Information required to be filed
I
children.text: that the absence from work is for reasons referred to in clause (i), and

children.label: II
children.text: the number of days for which there was such an absence.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a defined benefit plan, the employee’s accrued benefit determined under the plan and, except as provided in subsection (c)(3), expressed in the form of an annual benefit commencing at normal retirement age, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a plan which is not a defined benefit plan, the balance of the employee’s account.

children.label: i
children.text: a distribution of the present value of his entire nonforfeitable benefit if such distribution was in an amount (not more than the dollar limit under section 411(a)(11)(A)) permitted under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, or

children.label: ii
children.text: a distribution of the present value of his nonforfeitable benefit attributable to such service which he elected to receive.

children.label: i
children.text: received such a distribution in any plan year to which this section applies, which distribution was less than the present value of his accrued benefit,

children.label: ii
children.text: resumes employment covered under the plan, and

children.label: iii
children.text: repays the full amount of such distribution with, in the case of a defined benefit plan, interest at the rate determined for purposes of subsection (c)(2)(C).

children.heading: Accrued benefit attributable to employee contributions
children.label: D Accrued benefit attributable to employee contributions
children.text: The accrued benefit of an employee shall not be less than the amount determined under subsection (c)(2)(B) with respect to the employee’s accumulated contributions.

children.label: A
children.text: the time a plan participant attains normal retirement age under the plan, or

children.label: i
children.text: the time a plan participant attains age 65, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the 5th anniversary of the time a plan participant commenced participation in the plan.

children.label: A
children.text: medical benefits, and

children.label: B
children.text: disability benefits not in excess of the qualified disability benefit.

children.label: i
children.text: do not exceed such social security benefits, and

children.label: ii
children.text: terminate when such social security benefits commence.

children.heading: General rule
children.label: A General rule
children.text: A plan amendment changing any vesting schedule under the plan shall be treated as not satisfying the requirements of paragraph (2) if the nonforfeitable percentage of the accrued benefit derived from employer contributions (determined as of the later of the date such amendment is adopted, or the date such amendment becomes effective) of any employee who is a participant in the plan is less than such nonforfeitable percentage computed under the plan without regard to such amendment.

children.heading: Election of former schedule
children.label: B Election of former schedule
children.text: A plan amendment changing any vesting schedule under the plan shall be treated as not satisfying the requirements of paragraph (2) unless each participant having not less than 3 years of service is permitted to elect, within a reasonable period after the adoption of such amendment, to have his nonforfeitable percentage computed under the plan without regard to such amendment.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the present value of any nonforfeitable accrued benefit exceeds $7,000, a plan meets the requirements of this paragraph only if such plan provides that such benefit may not be immediately distributed without the consent of the participant.

children.heading: Determination of present value
children.label: B Determination of present value
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the present value shall be calculated in accordance with section 417(e)(3).

children.heading: Dividend distributions of ESOPS arrangement
children.label: C Dividend distributions of ESOPS arrangement
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to any distribution of dividends to which section 404(k) applies.

children.heading: Special rule for rollover contributions
children.label: D Special rule for rollover contributions
children.text: A plan shall not fail to meet the requirements of this paragraph if, under the terms of the plan, the present value of the nonforfeitable accrued benefit is determined without regard to that portion of such benefit which is attributable to rollover contributions (and earnings allocable thereto). For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “rollover contributions” means any rollover contribution under sections 402(c), 403(a)(4), 403(b)(8), 408(d)(3)(A)(ii), and 457(e)(16).

children.heading: Special rules for plans computing accrued benefits by reference to hypothetical account balance or equivalent amounts
children.label: 13 Special rules for plans computing accrued benefits by reference to hypothetical account balance or equivalent amounts
i
children.text: subject to subparagraph (B), the requirements of subsection (a)(2), or

children.label: ii
children.text: the requirements of subsection (a)(11) or (c), or the requirements of section 417(e), with respect to accrued benefits derived from employer contributions,

children.heading: 3-year vesting
children.label: B 3-year vesting
children.text: In the case of an applicable defined benefit plan, such plan shall be treated as meeting the requirements of subsection (a)(2) only if an employee who has completed at least 3 years of service has a nonforfeitable right to 100 percent of the employee’s accrued benefit derived from employer contributions.

children.heading: Applicable defined benefit plan and related rules
children.label: C Applicable defined benefit plan and related rules

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “applicable defined benefit plan” means a defined benefit plan under which the accrued benefit (or any portion thereof) is calculated as the balance of a hypothetical account maintained for the participant or as an accumulated percentage of the participant’s final average compensation.

children.heading: Regulations to include similar plans
children.label: ii Regulations to include similar plans
children.text: The Secretary shall issue regulations which include in the definition of an applicable defined benefit plan any defined benefit plan (or any portion of such a plan) which has an effect similar to an applicable defined benefit plan.

children.label: i
children.text: 3 percent of the normal retirement benefit to which he would be entitled if he commenced participation at the earliest possible entry age under the plan and served continuously until the earlier of age 65 or the normal retirement age specified under the plan, multiplied by

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of years (not in excess of 33⅓) of his participation in the plan.

children.label: i
children.text: any amendment to the plan which is in effect for the current year shall be treated as in effect for all other plan years;

children.label: ii
children.text: any change in an accrual rate which does not apply to any individual who is or could be a participant in the current year shall be disregarded;

children.label: iii
children.text: the fact that benefits under the plan may be payable to certain employees before normal retirement age shall be disregarded; and

children.label: iv
children.text: social security benefits and all other relevant factors used to compute benefits shall be treated as remaining constant as of the current year for all years after the current year.

children.heading: Fractional rule
children.label: C Fractional rule
children.text: A defined benefits plan satisfies the requirements of this paragraph if the accrued benefit to which any participant is entitled upon his separation from the service is not less than a fraction of the annual benefit commencing at normal retirement age to which he would be entitled under the plan as in effect on the date of his separation if he continued to earn annually until normal retirement age the same rate of compensation upon which his normal retirement benefit would be computed under the plan, determined as if he had attained normal retirement age on the date on which any such determination is made (but taking into account no more than the 10 years of service immediately preceding his separation from service). Such fraction shall be a fraction, not exceeding 1, the numerator of which is the total number of his years of participation in the plan (as of the date of his separation from the service) and the denominator of which is the total number of years he would have participated in the plan if he separated from the service at the normal retirement age. For purposes of this subparagraph, social security benefits and all other relevant factors used to compute benefits shall be treated as remaining constant as of the current year for all years after such current year.

children.heading: Accrual for service before effective date
children.label: D Accrual for service before effective date
i
children.text: his accrued benefit determined under the plan, as in effect from time to time prior to , orSeptember 2, 1974

children.label: ii
children.text: an accrued benefit which is not less than one-half of the accrued benefit to which such participant would have been entitled if subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) applied with respect to such years of participation.

children.heading: First two years of service
children.label: E First two years of service
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of this paragraph, a plan shall not be treated as not satisfying the requirements of this paragraph solely because the accrual of benefits under the plan does not become effective until the employee has two continuous years of service. For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “years of service” has the meaning provided by section 410(a)(3)(A).

children.heading: Certain insured defined benefit plans
children.label: F Certain insured defined benefit plans
i
children.text: is funded exclusively by the purchase of insurance contracts, and

children.label: ii
children.text: satisfies the requirements of subparagraphs (B) and (C) of section 412(e)(3) (relating to certain insurance contract plans),

children.heading: Accrued benefit may not decrease on account of increasing age or service
children.label: G Accrued benefit may not decrease on account of increasing age or service
i
children.text: do not exceed such social security benefits, and

children.label: ii
children.text: terminate when such social security benefits commence.

children.heading: Continued accrual beyond normal retirement age
children.label: H Continued accrual beyond normal retirement age

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Notwithstanding the preceding subparagraphs, a defined benefit plan shall be treated as not satisfying the requirements of this paragraph if, under the plan, an employee’s benefit accrual is ceased, or the rate of an employee’s benefit accrual is reduced, because of the attainment of any age.

children.heading: Certain limitations permitted
children.label: ii Certain limitations permitted
children.text: A plan shall not be treated as failing to meet the requirements of this subparagraph solely because the plan imposes (without regard to age) a limitation on the amount of benefits that the plan provides or a limitation on the number of years of service or years of participation which are taken into account for purposes of determining benefit accrual under the plan.

children.heading: Adjustments under plan for delayed retirement taken into account
children.label: iii Adjustments under plan for delayed retirement taken into account
I
children.text: if distribution of benefits under such plan with respect to such employee has commenced as of the end of such plan year, then any requirement of this subparagraph for continued accrual of benefits under such plan with respect to such employee during such plan year shall be treated as satisfied to the extent of the actuarial equivalent of inservice distribution of benefits, and

children.label: II
children.text: if distribution of benefits under such plan with respect to such employee has not commenced as of the end of such year in accordance with section 401(a)(14)(C), and the payment of benefits under such plan with respect to such employee is not suspended during such plan year pursuant to subsection (a)(3)(B), then any requirement of this subparagraph for continued accrual of benefits under such plan with respect to such employee during such plan year shall be treated as satisfied to the extent of any adjustment in the benefit payable under the plan during such plan year attributable to the delay in the distribution of benefits after the attainment of normal retirement age.

children.heading: Disregard of subsidized portion of early retirement benefit
children.label: iv Disregard of subsidized portion of early retirement benefit
children.text: A plan shall not be treated as failing to meet the requirements of clause (i) solely because the subsidized portion of any early retirement benefit is disregarded in determining benefit accruals.

children.heading: Coordination with other requirements
children.label: v Coordination with other requirements
children.text: The Secretary shall provide by regulation for the coordination of the requirements of this subparagraph with the requirements of subsection (a), sections 404, 410, and 415, and the provisions of this subchapter precluding discrimination in favor of highly compensated employees.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A defined contribution plan satisfies the requirements of this paragraph if, under the plan, allocations to the employee’s account are not ceased, and the rate at which amounts are allocated to the employee’s account is not reduced, because of the attainment of any age.

children.heading: Application to target benefit plans
children.label: B Application to target benefit plans
children.text: The Secretary shall provide by regulation for the application of the requirements of this paragraph to target benefit plans.

children.heading: Coordination with other requirements
children.label: C Coordination with other requirements
children.text: The Secretary may provide by regulation for the coordination of the requirements of this paragraph with the requirements of subsection (a), sections 404, 410, and 415, and the provisions of this subchapter precluding discrimination in favor of highly compensated employees.

children.heading: Separate accounting required in certain cases
children.label: 3 Separate accounting required in certain cases
A
children.text: in the case of the defined benefit plan, the plan requires separate accounting for the portion of each employee’s accrued benefit derived from any voluntary employee contributions permitted under the plan; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of any plan which is not a defined benefit plan, the plan requires separate accounting for each employee’s accrued benefit.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: A Definition
children.text: For purposes of determining an employee’s accrued benefit, the term “year of participation” means a period of service (beginning at the earliest date on which the employee is a participant in the plan and which is included in a period of service required to be taken into account under section 410(a)(5), determined without regard to section 410(a)(5)(E)) as determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Labor which provide for the calculation of such period on any reasonable and consistent basis.

children.heading: Less than full time service
children.label: B Less than full time service
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, except as provided in subparagraph (C), in the case of any employee whose customary employment is less than full time, the calculation of such employee’s service on any basis which provides less than a ratable portion of the accrued benefit to which he would be entitled under the plan if his customary employment were full time shall not be treated as made on a reasonable and consistent basis.

children.heading: Less than 1,000 hours of service during year
children.label: C Less than 1,000 hours of service during year
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, in the case of any employee whose service is less than 1,000 hours during any calendar year, plan year or other 12-consecutive month period designated by the plan (and not prohibited under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Labor) the calculation of his period of service shall not be treated as not made on a reasonable and consistent basis solely because such service is not taken into account.

children.heading: Seasonal industries
children.label: D Seasonal industries
children.text: In the case of any seasonal industry where the customary period of employment is less than 1,000 hours during a calendar year, the term “year of participation” shall be such period as determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Labor.

children.heading: Maritime industries
children.label: E Maritime industries
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, in the case of any maritime industry, 125 days of service shall be treated as a year of participation. The Secretary of Labor may prescribe regulations to carry out the purposes of this subparagraph.

children.heading: Comparison to similarly situated younger individual
children.label: A Comparison to similarly situated younger individual

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A plan shall not be treated as failing to meet the requirements of paragraph (1)(H)(i) if a participant’s accrued benefit, as determined as of any date under the terms of the plan, would be equal to or greater than that of any similarly situated, younger individual who is or could be a participant.

children.heading: Similarly situated
children.label: ii Similarly situated
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, a participant is similarly situated to any other individual if such participant is identical to such other individual in every respect (including period of service, compensation, position, date of hire, work history, and any other respect) except for age.

children.heading: Disregard of subsidized early retirement benefits
children.label: iii Disregard of subsidized early retirement benefits
children.text: In determining the accrued benefit as of any date for purposes of this subparagraph, the subsidized portion of any early retirement benefit or retirement-type subsidy shall be disregarded.

children.heading: Accrued benefit
children.label: iv Accrued benefit
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the accrued benefit may, under the terms of the plan, be expressed as an annuity payable at normal retirement age, the balance of a hypothetical account, or the current value of the accumulated percentage of the employee’s final average compensation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: An applicable defined benefit plan shall be treated as failing to meet the requirements of paragraph (1)(H) unless the terms of the plan provide that any interest credit (or an equivalent amount) for any plan year shall be at a rate which is not greater than a market rate of return. A plan shall not be treated as failing to meet the requirements of this subclause merely because the plan provides for a reasonable minimum guaranteed rate of return or for a rate of return that is equal to the greater of a fixed or variable rate of return.

children.heading: Preservation of capital
children.label: II Preservation of capital
children.text: An applicable defined benefit plan shall be treated as failing to meet the requirements of paragraph (1)(H) unless the plan provides that an interest credit (or equivalent amount) of less than zero shall in no event result in the account balance or similar amount being less than the aggregate amount of contributions credited to the account.

children.heading: Market rate of return
children.label: III Market rate of return
children.text: The Secretary may provide by regulation for rules governing the calculation of a market rate of return for purposes of subclause (I) and for permissible methods of crediting interest to the account (including fixed or variable interest rates) resulting in effective rates of return meeting the requirements of subclause (I).

children.heading: Special rule for plan conversions
children.label: ii Special rule for plan conversions
children.text: If, after , an applicable plan amendment is adopted, the plan shall be treated as failing to meet the requirements of paragraph (1)(H) unless the requirements of clause (iii) are met with respect to each individual who was a participant in the plan immediately before the adoption of the amendment.June 29, 2005

children.label: I
children.text: the participant’s accrued benefit for years of service before the effective date of the amendment, determined under the terms of the plan as in effect before the amendment, plus

children.label: II
children.text: the participant’s accrued benefit for years of service after the effective date of the amendment, determined under the terms of the plan as in effect after the amendment.

children.heading: Special rules for early retirement subsidies
children.label: iv Special rules for early retirement subsidies
children.text: For purposes of clause (iii)(I), the plan shall credit the accumulation account or similar amount with the amount of any early retirement benefit or retirement-type subsidy for the plan year in which the participant retires if, as of such time, the participant has met the age, years of service, and other requirements under the plan for entitlement to such benefit or subsidy.1So in original. Probably should be “similar account”.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The term “applicable plan amendment” means an amendment to a defined benefit plan which has the effect of converting the plan to an applicable defined benefit plan.

children.heading: Special rule for coordinated benefits
children.label: II Special rule for coordinated benefits
children.text: If the benefits of 2 or more defined benefit plans established or maintained by an employer are coordinated in such a manner as to have the effect of the adoption of an amendment described in subclause (I), the sponsor of the defined benefit plan or plans providing for such coordination shall be treated as having adopted such a plan amendment as of the date such coordination begins.

children.heading: Multiple amendments
children.label: III Multiple amendments
children.text: The Secretary shall issue regulations to prevent the avoidance of the purposes of this subparagraph through the use of 2 or more plan amendments rather than a single amendment.

children.heading: Applicable defined benefit plan
children.label: IV Applicable defined benefit plan
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “applicable defined benefit plan” has the meaning given such term by section 411(a)(13).

children.label: I
children.text: if the interest credit rate (or an equivalent amount) under the plan is a variable rate, the rate of interest used to determine accrued benefits under the plan shall be equal to the average of the rates of interest used under the plan during the 5-year period ending on the termination date, and

children.label: II
children.text: the interest rate and mortality table used to determine the amount of any benefit under the plan payable in the form of an annuity payable at normal retirement age shall be the rate and table specified under the plan for such purpose as of the termination date, except that if such interest rate is a variable rate, the interest rate shall be determined under the rules of subclause (I).

children.heading: Certain offsets permitted
children.label: C Certain offsets permitted
children.text: A plan shall not be treated as failing to meet the requirements of paragraph (1)(H)(i) solely because the plan provides offsets against benefits under the plan to the extent such offsets are otherwise allowable in applying the requirements of section 401(a).

children.heading: Permitted disparities in plan contributions or benefits
children.label: D Permitted disparities in plan contributions or benefits
children.text: A plan shall not be treated as failing to meet the requirements of paragraph (1)(H) solely because the plan provides a disparity in contributions or benefits with respect to which the requirements of section 401() are met.l

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A plan shall not be treated as failing to meet the requirements of paragraph (1)(H) solely because the plan provides for indexing of accrued benefits under the plan.

children.heading: Protection against loss
children.label: ii Protection against loss
children.text: Except in the case of any benefit provided in the form of a variable annuity, clause (i) shall not apply with respect to any indexing which results in an accrued benefit less than the accrued benefit determined without regard to such indexing.

children.heading: Indexing
children.label: iii Indexing
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “indexing” means, in connection with an accrued benefit, the periodic adjustment of the accrued benefit by means of the application of a recognized investment index or methodology.

children.heading: Early retirement benefit or retirement-type subsidy
children.label: F Early retirement benefit or retirement-type subsidy
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the terms “early retirement benefit” and “retirement-type subsidy” have the meaning given such terms in subsection (d)(6)(B)(i).

children.heading: Benefit accrued to date
children.label: G Benefit accrued to date
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, any reference to the accrued benefit shall be a reference to such benefit accrued to date.

children.heading: Projected interest crediting rate
children.label: 6 Projected interest crediting rate
children.text: For purposes of subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of paragraph (1), in the case of an applicable defined benefit plan (as defined in subsection (a)(13)(C)) which provides variable interest crediting rates, the interest crediting rate which is treated as in effect and as the projected interest crediting rate shall be a reasonable projection of such variable interest crediting rate, not to exceed 6 percent.

children.heading: Allocation of accrued benefits between employer and employee contributions
children.label: c Allocation of accrued benefits between employer and employee contributions

children.heading: Accrued benefit derived from employer contributions
children.label: 1 Accrued benefit derived from employer contributions
children.text: For purposes of this section, an employee’s accrued benefit derived from employer contributions as of any applicable date is the excess, if any, of the accrued benefit for such employee as of such applicable date over the accrued benefit derived from contributions made by such employee as of such date.

children.heading: Accrued benefit derived from employee contributions
children.label: 2 Accrued benefit derived from employee contributions

children.heading: Plans other than defined benefit plans
children.label: A Plans other than defined benefit plans
i
children.text: except as provided in clause (ii), the balance of the employee’s separate account consisting only of his contributions and the income, expenses, gains, and losses attributable thereto, or

children.label: ii
children.text: if a separate account is not maintained with respect to an employee’s contributions under such a plan, the amount which bears the same ratio to his total accrued benefit as the total amount of the employee’s contributions (less withdrawals) bears to the sum of such contributions and the contributions made on his behalf by the employer (less withdrawals).

children.heading: Defined benefit plans
children.label: B Defined benefit plans
children.text: In the case of a defined benefit plan, the accrued benefit derived from contributions made by an employee as of any applicable date is the amount equal to the employee’s accumulated contributions expressed as an annual benefit commencing at normal retirement age, using an interest rate which would be used under the plan under section 417(e)(3) (as of the determination date).

children.label: i
children.text: all mandatory contributions made by the employee,

children.label: ii
children.text: interest (if any) under the plan to the end of the last plan year to which subsection (a)(2) does not apply (by reason of the applicable effective date), and

children.label: I
children.text: at the rate of 120 percent of the Federal mid-term rate (as in effect under section 1274 for the 1st month of a plan year) for the period beginning with the 1st plan year to which subsection (a)(2) applies (by reason of the applicable effective date) and ending with the date on which the determination is being made, and

children.label: II
children.text: at the interest rate which would be used under the plan under section 417(e)(3) (as of the determination date) for the period beginning with the determination date and ending on the date on which the employee attains normal retirement age.

children.heading: Adjustments
children.label: D Adjustments
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to adjust by regulation the conversion factor described in subparagraph (B) from time to time as he may deem necessary. No such adjustment shall be effective for a plan year beginning before the expiration of 1 year after such adjustment is determined and published.

children.heading: Actuarial adjustment
children.label: 3 Actuarial adjustment
children.text: For purposes of this section, in the case of any defined benefit plan, if an employee’s accrued benefit is to be determined as an amount other than an annual benefit commencing at normal retirement age, or if the accrued benefit derived from contributions made by an employee is to be determined with respect to a benefit other than an annual benefit in the form of a single life annuity (without ancillary benefits) commencing at normal retirement age, the employee’s accrued benefit, or the accrued benefits derived from contributions made by an employee, as the case may be, shall be the actuarial equivalent of such benefit or amount determined under paragraph (1) or (2).

children.label: A
children.text: there has been a pattern of abuse under the plan (such as a dismissal of employees before their accrued benefits become nonforfeitable) tending to discriminate in favor of employees who are highly compensated employees (within the meaning of section 414(q)), or

children.label: B
children.text: there have been, or there is reason to believe there will be, an accrual of benefits or forfeitures tending to discriminate in favor of employees who are highly compensated employees (within the meaning of section 414(q)).

children.heading: Prohibited discrimination
children.label: 2 Prohibited discrimination
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to benefits which may not be provided for designated employees in the event of early termination of the plan under provisions of the plan adopted pursuant to regulations prescribed by the Secretary to preclude the discrimination prohibited by section 401(a)(4).

children.heading: Termination or partial termination; discontinuance of contributions
children.label: 3 Termination or partial termination; discontinuance of contributions
A
children.text: upon its termination or partial termination, or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a plan to which section 412 does not apply, upon complete discontinuance of contributions under the plan,

children.heading: Treatment of voluntary employee contributions
children.label: 5 Treatment of voluntary employee contributions
children.text: In the case of a defined benefit plan which permits voluntary employee contributions, the portion of an employee’s accrued benefit derived from such contributions shall be treated as an accrued benefit derived from employee contributions under a plan other than a defined benefit plan.

children.heading: Accrued benefit not to be decreased by amendment
children.label: 6 Accrued benefit not to be decreased by amendment

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A plan shall be treated as not satisfying the requirements of this section if the accrued benefit of a participant is decreased by an amendment of the plan, other than an amendment described in section 412(d)(2), or section 4281 of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974.

children.heading: Treatment of certain plan amendments
children.label: B Treatment of certain plan amendments
i
children.text: eliminating or reducing an early retirement benefit or a retirement-type subsidy (as defined in regulations), or

children.label: ii
children.text: eliminating an optional form of benefit,

children.label: i
children.text: tax credit employee stock ownership plan (as defined in section 409(a)), or

children.label: ii
children.text: employee stock ownership plan (as defined in section 4975(e)(7)),

children.label: I
children.text: the forms of distribution previously available under the transferor plan applied to the account of a participant or beneficiary under the transferor plan that was transferred from the transferor plan to the transferee plan pursuant to a direct transfer rather than pursuant to a distribution from the transferor plan,

children.label: II
children.text: the terms of both the transferor plan and the transferee plan authorize the transfer described in subclause (I),

children.label: III
children.text: the transfer described in subclause (I) was made pursuant to a voluntary election by the participant or beneficiary whose account was transferred to the transferee plan,

children.label: IV
children.text: the election described in subclause (III) was made after the participant or beneficiary received a notice describing the consequences of making the election, and

children.label: V
children.text: the transferee plan allows the participant or beneficiary described in subclause (III) to receive any distribution to which the participant or beneficiary is entitled under the transferee plan in the form of a single sum distribution.

children.heading: Special rule for mergers, etc.
children.label: ii Special rule for mergers, etc.
children.text: Clause (i) shall apply to plan mergers and other transactions having the effect of a direct transfer, including consolidations of benefits attributable to different employers within a multiple employer plan.

children.heading: Elimination of form of distribution
children.label: E Elimination of form of distribution
i
children.text: a single sum payment is available to such participant at the same time or times as the form of distribution being eliminated, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such single sum payment is based on the same or greater portion of the participant’s account as the form of distribution being eliminated.

children.heading: Application of vesting standards to certain plans
children.label: e Application of vesting standards to certain plans
A
children.text: a governmental plan (within the meaning of section 414(d)),

children.label: B
children.text: a church plan (within the meaning of section 414(e)) with respect to which the election provided by section 410(d) has not been made,

children.label: C
children.text: a plan which has not, at any time after , provided for employer contributions, andSeptember 2, 1974

children.label: D
children.text: a plan established and maintained by a society, order, or association described in section 501(c)(8) or (9), if no part of the contributions to or under such plan are made by employers of participants in such plan.

A plan described in paragraph (1) shall be treated as meeting the requirements of this section, for purposes of section 401(a), if such plan meets the vesting requirements resulting from the application of sections 401(a)(4) and 401(a)(7) as in effect on .September 1, 1974

children.heading: Special rule for determining normal retirement age for certain existing defined benefit plans
children.label: f Special rule for determining normal retirement age for certain existing defined benefit plans

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a)(8), an applicable plan shall not be treated as failing to meet any requirement of this subchapter, or as failing to have a uniform normal retirement age for purposes of this subchapter, solely because the plan provides for a normal retirement age described in paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: an age otherwise permitted under subsection (a)(8), or

children.label: ii
children.text: the age at which a participant completes the number of years (not less than 30 years) of benefit accrual service specified by the plan.

children.heading: Expanded application
children.label: B Expanded application
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (C), if, after , an applicable plan is amended to expand the application of the normal retirement age described in subparagraph (A) to additional participants or to employees of additional employers maintaining the plan, such plan shall also be treated as an applicable plan with respect to such participants or employees.December 8, 2014

children.label: ii
children.text: is an employee at any time on or before , of any employer maintaining the plan, and who becomes a participant in such plan after such date.January 1, 2017
